figures.caption: Figure 1. Overview of the Med-CAM framework. Given an input medical image x, a lightweight U-Net produces a binary mask m, yielding the masked explanation e = m ⊙ x. The mask highlights the minimal diagnostic evidence required to preserve the model’s decision.
Figure 2. Med-CAM explanations on BACH H&E breast cancer histopathology slides using a ViT-16 classifier.
Figure 3. Med-CAM explanations on brain tumor MRI (left) and IDRiD retinal fundus images (right).
Figure 4. Med-CAM vs Grad-CAM on HAM10000.
